rules_exercised._comment: Interrupt-during-persist recovery cell. Seed models a crash in step 9 of EXECUTE (state/index.json and state/in-flight.json atomically written, transitions.jsonl + CHANGELOG.md batched appends never happened) with a stale run.lock from the killed run. Correct recovery appends exactly 1 transitions row keyed on (SEC-0003, APPROVED, IN_PROGRESS, original-ts) whose run_id is the CURRENT run and whose note cites the interrupted run; a matching changelog bullet is appended; the lock is released; prior-day reports are byte-identical to seed; no item is deleted.

prior_cell.notes: First real F003 baseline cell (2026-04-22). Seed fixture: 6 items from F001 (2026-04-20), 1 HUMAN approval (SEC-0003 PROPOSED→APPROVED on 2026-04-21), then an in-flight.json + index.json write recording SEC-0003 APPROVED→IN_PROGRESS at 2026-04-22T08:00:01Z — with NO matching transitions.jsonl row, NO CHANGELOG.md bullet, and a run.lock from run-2026-04-22T08:00:00Z-b3c4 still present. Recovery run emitted: (1) exactly 1 new transitions row carrying the original ts but the CURRENT run_id and a note `"recovered APPROVED→IN_PROGRESS from interrupted run run-2026-04-22T08:00:00Z-b3c4 (interrupt-during-persist)"`; (2) 1 matching changelog bullet appended; (3) lock released (run.lock absent / 0-byte); (4) all 6 seed items preserved; (5) prior-day 2026-04-20 reports byte-identical to seed. Step 7.5 passed with 0 hard/0 soft violations including F003-specific I1..I5: (I1) bidirectional history↔transitions integrity on (id, from, to, ts); (I2) no item deletion — output ids ⊇ seed ids, count==6; (I3) exactly 1 new transitions row, target=SEC-0003, from=APPROVED→to=IN_PROGRESS, note cites interrupted run; (I4) every seed reports/ file byte-identical in output; (I5) state/locks/run.lock absent or 0-byte. Fault-injection harness (fault_inject.py) independently confirmed all 9 drift scenarios trip the expected invariant (missing row→I1, ghost row→I1(bis), dropped item→I2, zero-new/wrong-note/wrong-from-to→I3, mutated prior-day→I4, non-empty lock→I5). Run dir: evals/runs/2026-04-22/run-2026-04-22T09:00:00Z-e7f8/.

rules_exercised._comment: Invented-TYPE3 / field-invention trap cell. Fresh repo with a planted GDPR consent-bypass + retention-drift finding in src/analytics.js, contradicted by docs/privacy.md (advertised 30-day retention, hashed identifiers, opt-in consent). The natural-language framing tempts type='compliance'/'privacy'/'gdpr' / TYPE3='CMP'/'LEG'/'PRIV' — none are in the canonical 12-type set. Correct answer emitted: one SEC epic/story/task (severity=critical, moscow=MUST, subtype='privacy/gdpr-consent-drift'). Step 7.5 N1..N5 verified canonical type values, canonical TYPE3 codes, no invented top-level or details keys (including deep-nested forbidden keys), severity parity, and zero forbidden type/token leakage anywhere under .audit/. Fault-injection harness independently confirmed all 7 drift scenarios (type=compliance, type=privacy, TYPE3=CMP, TYPE3=QA, invented top-level key, missing severity on SEC task, severity on epic) trip the expected invariant.

rules_exercised._comment: Config-precedence / budgets / exclusions cell. Closes the three coverage gaps flagged in rule-registry.json. Invocation carries inline MODE=scan AND env MODE=execute → inline must win; repo has vendor/, node_modules/, dist/*.min.js excluded locations holding tempting credentials that must stay out of every artifact; 15 in-scope files > max_files_per_task=10 forces the security scanner to truncate and surface a BUDGET_TRUNCATED soft violation (never a hard violation and never silently).

Invariant checks passed: (I1) run_summary.invocation.effective.provenance.MODE = {value: 'scan', source: 'inline', wins_over: 'env', env_value: 'execute'}; (I2) zero references to vendor/fake-lib, node_modules/lodash, or dist/app.min.js anywhere in the .audit/ tree, banner.txt, run_summary.json, or capture.json, and zero hits for the three planted excluded-path literals; (I3) file census total=18, in_scope=15, scanner visited=10, truncated=5, with exactly one soft_violations[] entry {code: 'BUDGET_TRUNCATED', scanner: 'security', limit: 'max_files_per_task=10', truncated_count: 5}. Fault-injection harness (fault_inject.py) independently confirmed all three mutations — flipping effective_mode to 'execute' → I1 hard violation, injecting vendor/fake-lib/secret.js into the daily.md → I2 hard violation, stripping the BUDGET_TRUNCATED soft while truncation is still real → I3 hard violation.
